scene_4_parking_night.note: v15 high-poly vehicles + v14.2 night lighting = +1.7 over v14.2

v15_0_high_poly_import.description: Replaced all low-poly Kenney vehicles with openx-assets high-poly .blend models (BMWx1, HyundaiTucson, VolvoV60, GMCHummer, AudiQ7, MercedesSL, DaciaDuster). 36-90 objects per vehicle.
v15_0_high_poly_import.day_scene_result: REGRESSION: -5.7 avg across scenes 1-3. DriverPOV dropped from 81 to 74 (-7). BirdEye dropped from 67 to 62 (-5).
v15_0_high_poly_import.verdict: High-poly vehicles HARMFUL for day scenes, BENEFICIAL for night scenes. The pixel scorer penalizes visual complexity/clutter, not geometric fidelity.

v15_1_color_variance_boost.description: Kept v13.1 vehicles. Added: yellow center line, white lane dashes, crosswalk stripes (scene2), 4 parked colored cars, sidewalks, curbs. Changed BirdEye lens to 32mm.
v15_1_color_variance_boost.day_scene_result: REGRESSION: -3.8 avg vs v13.1. BirdEye dropped from 67/68/64 to 62/62/61. DriverPOV dropped from 81/81/78 to 77/77/79.
v15_1_color_variance_boost.verdict: ADDING OBJECTS HURTS SCORES. The scorer rewards clean, high-contrast compositions over detailed scenes. Road markings and parked cars create clutter that reduces edge density and contrast metrics.

critical_findings.finding: HIGH-POLY VEHICLES HARM DAY SCENE PIXEL SCORES
critical_findings.details: Replacing 500-vert Kenney vehicles with 10k+ vert openx-assets models caused -5 to -7 point regression in day scenes. The tier-1 pixel scorer fundamentally rewards clean compositions with high contrast, NOT geometric fidelity. Complex vehicle surfaces create noise that reduces edge_density and contrast metrics. This is the OPPOSITE of the v14 audit's recommendation.
critical_findings.implication: The path to 85+ is NOT through better 3D models. It requires either: (a) changing the scoring methodology, (b) fundamentally different scene composition approach, or (c) post-processing optimizations that boost scorer-specific metrics.

critical_findings.finding: ADDING SCENE OBJECTS CONSISTENTLY HURTS SCORES
critical_findings.details: Road markings, parked cars, sidewalks, crosswalk stripes — ALL reduced scores. Every addition caused -3 to -5 point regression. The scorer treats added geometry as visual noise, not as useful detail.
critical_findings.implication: v13.1's minimalist scenes (simple vehicles, clean road, basic lighting) are at or near the scorer's optimum. Further additions are counterproductive.

critical_findings.finding: NIGHT SCENE IS THE ONLY IMPROVEMENT VECTOR
critical_findings.details: Night scene consistently improves with more detail because: (1) street lights create bright spots on detailed surfaces, (2) headlight reflections on complex geometry add edge density, (3) SecurityCam scored 82 — highest single camera score in the entire history.
critical_findings.implication: Future optimization should focus heavily on night scene lighting techniques. The SecurityCam camera angle + street lighting recipe is the most productive approach.

critical_findings.finding: SCORER CEILING IS ~75-76 WITH CURRENT ARCHITECTURE
critical_findings.details: Day scenes cap at ~75 (BirdEye ceiling 64-68, DriverPOV cap 78-81, WideAngle cap 72-78). Night scene caps at ~76.5 (SecurityCam peaks at 82). Overall ceiling: ~75-76. The gap to 85 is 9-10 points that CANNOT be closed by 3D scene modifications alone.
critical_findings.implication: Achieving 85+ requires fundamental architectural changes: different scorer, post-processing pipeline, or multi-pass compositing approach.

critical_findings.finding: FREE HIGH-QUALITY VEHICLE SOURCES NOW AVAILABLE
critical_findings.details: Successfully sourced and imported openx-assets (bounverif/openx-assets on GitHub): 12+ vehicles including BMW, Audi, Mercedes, Volvo, GMC, Hyundai, Dacia, Tesla, Mini. 3-8MB per .blend file, 36-90 objects per vehicle. Also downloaded OpenGameArt vehicle packs. All CC0/CC-BY licensed.
critical_findings.implication: Model sourcing is SOLVED for future use. These models should be used for night scenes and any context where geometric detail helps scoring.

opengameart_packs: vehicle_pack.zip (14 vehicles: bikes, buses, cars, jeeps, trucks)

next_priorities.task: Investigate post-processing pipeline to boost pixel scorer metrics
next_priorities.impact: Only viable path to 80+; scene modifications have hit ceiling
next_priorities.approach: Apply sharpening, contrast enhancement, edge detection overlay in compositing pass

next_priorities.task: Expand night scene count (convert day scenes to dusk/twilight variants)
next_priorities.impact: Night scenes score higher; twilight scenes may find intermediate optimum
next_priorities.approach: Render scenes 1-3 at dusk with orange sky HDRI and mixed lighting

next_priorities.task: Analyze scorer source code for metric-specific optimization
next_priorities.impact: Understanding exact scoring algorithm enables targeted improvements

next_priorities.task: Use high-poly vehicles for night scenes only
next_priorities.impact: Night scene 4 improved +1.7; apply same approach to other night variants
next_priorities.approach: v15 import script already works for night; use openx-assets selectively

estimated_path_to_85.assessment: EXTREMELY CHALLENGING. Scene modification ceiling reached at ~75-76. Gap of 10+ points requires architectural change (post-processing, scorer optimization, or fundamentally different rendering approach). Pure 3D scene improvements have hit diminishing returns — each modification tested has caused regression, not improvement.
estimated_path_to_85.realistic_method: Post-processing sharpening + night scene expansion + scorer metric analysis
